reasoning:
The finding is incorrectly classified as reflected XSS and the sink is not reachable in the Lite plugin under normal conditions. Detailed analysis:

1. WRONG VULN CLASS: $settings['title'] is read from a WordPress option (get_option via WC_Order_Export_Manage::get()), not from the current HTTP request. The bulk request only supplies export_bulk_profile and ids. This would be stored XSS at best, not reflected XSS.

2. SINK UNREACHABLE IN LITE: The die($output) branch at line 61 fires only when $browser_output is false. $browser_output = empty($settings['destination']['not_download_browser']). In the Lite plugin: (a) show_destination_in_profile defaults to false so $destination_flag is false; (b) $settings is built from array_merge(get_defaults_filters(), get_settings_from_bulk_request()) — neither source sets destination.not_download_browser; (c) the default destination array only contains zapier_export_type. Therefore $browser_output is always true in Lite, meaning send_headers() and send_contents_delete_file() are called (streaming the file to browser), and the die($output) path is never reached.

3. SECOND CONDITION ALSO BLOCKS: Even if $browser_output were false, the code does wp_safe_redirect(HTTP_REFERER) first when HTTP_REFERER is present. Any browser-initiated request will have HTTP_REFERER, so die($output) is only reached in non-browser/direct AJAX calls with no referer.

4. $result['text'] NON-ISSUE: build_and_send_file() only populates $result via WC_Order_Export_Pro_Engine::export() when $export (the $destination_flag) is truthy. In Lite, $destination_flag is always false, so $result is always [] and implode produces an empty string.

5. AUTH IS CORRECT: ajax_gate() does require view_woocommerce_reports or cap_export_orders — so the endpoint is admin/shop-manager only. The nonce check in ajax_gate() applies to the tab branch; the global handler branch (used here) lacks check_admin_referer, but capability check remains.

Conclusion: The die($output) sink with unescaped $settings['title'] is real code, but the path to it is blocked in Lite by $browser_output always being true. The vuln class label is wrong (stored not reflected). The finding as stated does not represent an exploitable vulnerability in the Lite plugin.
